title: [InstCombinePHI] Enable gep arg fold transform with constant indices
author: OutOfCache
description:
https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/DzO5Ow

The fold changes a phi node with getelementptrs as incoming values to a phi node with offsets, while creating a new getelementptr after the phi and the phi value as offset:

```llvm
; before
%old.phi = phi ptr [ %gep0, %bb0], [ %gep1, %bb1]

; after
%new.phi = phi i64 [ %off0, %bb0], [ %off1, %bb1 ]
%new.gep = getelementptr i8, ptr %p, i64 %new.phi
```
This transform should not happen if we index into a struct, since it creates a new gep with variable index and structs need constant indices.

Previously, this was prevented by disabling the transform for any constant indices. Now, the change should apply to getelementptrs with constant indices, if the indexed type is not a struct.

Do not transform if the offset is constant for some of the geps and variable for others, as suggested here https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/169919

tests.test_body:
define ptr @gep_mixed_offset_1(i1 %cond, ptr %ptr, i64 %off1) {
entry:
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr, i64 16
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr, i64 %off1
  br i1 %cond, label %f1, label %f0

f0:                                               ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

f1:                                               ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %f1, %f0
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep0, %f0 ], [ %gep1, %f1 ]
  ret ptr %phi
}

define ptr @gep_constant_offset(i1 %cond, ptr %ptr) {
entry:
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr, i64 16
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr, i64 32
  br i1 %cond, label %f1, label %f0

f0:                                               ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

f1:                                               ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %f1, %f0
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep0, %f0 ], [ %gep1, %f1 ]
  ret ptr %phi
}

%foo = type { i16, i16, i16 }

define ptr @struct_constant_offset(i1 %cond, %foo %ptr) {
entry:
  %lane.0 = alloca %foo, align 16
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds %foo, ptr %lane.0, i64 1
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds %foo, ptr %lane.0, i64 2
  br i1 %cond, label %f1, label %f0

f0:                                               ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

f1:                                               ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %f1, %f0
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep0, %f0 ], [ %gep1, %f1 ]
  ret ptr %phi
}

%foo = type { i16, i16, i16 }

define <16 x ptr> @test(i1 %tobool, i1 %end, <16 x i64> %varidx) {
entry:
  %lane.0 = alloca %foo, align 16
  %lane.15 = insertelement <16 x ptr> poison, ptr %lane.0, i64 0
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %f1, %f0, %entry
  %vec.phi = phi <16 x ptr> [ %mm_vectorGEP1, %f0 ], [ %mm_vectorGEP2, %f1 ], [ zeroinitializer, %entry ]
  %mm_vectorGEP1 = getelementptr inbounds %foo, <16 x ptr> %lane.15, <16 x i64> %varidx, <16 x i32> splat (i32 1)
  %mm_vectorGEP2 = getelementptr inbounds %foo, <16 x ptr> %lane.15, <16 x i64> %varidx, <16 x i32> splat (i32 2)
  br i1 %tobool, label %f1, label %f0

f0:                                               ; preds = %loop
  br i1 %end, label %loop, label %merge

f1:                                               ; preds = %loop
  br i1 %end, label %loop, label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %f1, %f0
  ret <16 x ptr> %vec.phi
}

define ptr @gep_different_offset_types_canonicalized(i1 %cond, i1 %end, ptr %ptr, i64 %off0, i32 %off1) {
entry:
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds <3 x i8>, ptr %ptr, i64 0, i64 %off0
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds <3 x i8>, ptr %ptr, i64 0, i32 %off1
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %f1, %f0, %entry
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep0, %f0 ], [ %gep1, %f1 ], [ %gep0, %entry ]
  br i1 %cond, label %f1, label %f0

f0:                                               ; preds = %loop
  br i1 %end, label %merge, label %loop

f1:                                               ; preds = %loop
  br i1 %end, label %merge, label %loop

merge:                                            ; preds = %f1, %f0
  ret ptr %phi
}

define ptr @gep_mixed_offset_2(i1 %cond, ptr %ptr, i64 %off0) {
entry:
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr, i64 %off0
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr, i64 32
  br i1 %cond, label %f1, label %f0

f0:                                               ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

f1:                                               ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %f1, %f0
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep0, %f0 ], [ %gep1, %f1 ]
  ret ptr %phi
}

define ptr @gep_different_offset_types_negative(i1 %cond, i1 %end, ptr %ptr, i64 %off0, i32 %off1) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %f1, %f0, %entry
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep0, %f0 ], [ %gep1, %f1 ], [ null, %entry ]
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds <3 x i8>, ptr %ptr, i64 0, i64 %off0
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds <3 x i8>, ptr %ptr, i64 0, i32 %off1
  br i1 %cond, label %f1, label %f0

f0:                                               ; preds = %loop
  br i1 %end, label %merge, label %loop

f1:                                               ; preds = %loop
  br i1 %end, label %merge, label %loop

merge:                                            ; preds = %f1, %f0
  ret ptr %phi
}

define ptr @gep_variable_offset(i1 %cond, ptr %ptr, i64 %off0, i64 %off1) {
entry:
  %gep0 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr, i64 %off0
  %gep1 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %ptr, i64 %off1
  br i1 %cond, label %f1, label %f0

f0:                                               ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

f1:                                               ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %f1, %f0
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep0, %f0 ], [ %gep1, %f1 ]
  ret ptr %phi
}

define ptr @phi_of_gep_different_type(i1 %c, ptr %p) {
  br i1 %c, label %if, label %else

if:                                               ; preds = %0
  %gep1 = getelementptr i32, ptr %p, i64 1
  br label %join

else:                                             ; preds = %0
  %gep2 = getelementptr i64, ptr %p, i64 1
  br label %join

join:                                             ; preds = %else, %if
  %phi = phi ptr [ %gep1, %if ], [ %gep2, %else ]
  ret ptr %phi
}
